steps.title: Identify what the conclusion requires
steps.content: The team concluded snakes transmit BovB to frogs via HT, either directly or indirectly—puzzling because frogs don't survive snake encounters. The most direct support would identify a mechanism connecting snakes and frogs without requiring frog survival.

steps.title: Match the mechanism to the conclusion
steps.content: Parasites that feed on both snakes and frogs while carrying BovB create a biological vector for indirect transmission (snakes → parasites → frogs), solving the paradox of how frogs acquire the gene without surviving encounters. The other choices either address frog-to-frog transfer (A), rule out a potential indirect pathway (C), or discuss fitness rather than transmission mechanism (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
